content_hints.description: The Ruin Entrance yawns open where the mountainside collapsed millennia ago, revealing architecture that should not exist -- walls of seamless stone with no mortar joints, no chisel marks, no evidence of human construction at all. The stone is warm despite the mountain chill, and when you press your palm to it, you feel a vibration like a held note.

npcs.name: Archaeologist Rushu
npcs.dialogue: These ruins predate the Shin. They predate the Desolations. Whatever built this place was working with principles of Surgebinding we have no name for. Touch nothing with exposed skin. The last team that did lost three days they cannot account for.

event_triggers: On entry: Glyphs carved into the threshold illuminate as you cross -- a sequence that matches your heartbeat for three pulses, then diverges into a rhythm you do not recognize

content_hints.description: The Collapsed Gallery was once a corridor of considerable grandeur -- vaulted ceiling, pillared walls, floor inlaid with metals that still gleam despite the ages. Half of it has fallen in, stone blocks the size of carriages tumbled across the path. Between the rubble, murals survive on the remaining walls: figures wielding Surges, but with anatomies that are not quite human.
event_triggers: On entry: A construct embedded in the rubble activates -- one arm free, the other pinned beneath a fallen pillar. Its crystal eye focuses on you and emits a sound that might be a greeting or a threat. The language has been dead for four thousand years.

content_hints.description: The Inscription Hall. Every surface -- floor, walls, ceiling -- is covered in text carved at varying depths, as if the builders ran out of surfaces and simply carved over older inscriptions. The languages shift every few feet: Dawnchant, proto-Shin, and three scripts no living scholar can identify. Stormlight-infused gemstones set into the walls provide illumination, still burning after millennia.

npcs.name: Spren Scholar (Inkspren)
npcs.dialogue: These inscriptions describe a war fought before the Oathpact. Not the Desolations you know -- something older. Something the Heralds themselves chose to forget. I can translate fragments, but the truth in them may be more dangerous than the ruins.

event_triggers: On entry: As you read one inscription, the text on the adjacent wall rearranges itself -- the ruins are responding to which passages are being studied

content_hints.description: The Spren Binding Chamber is a circular room with ten alcoves arranged around a central platform. Each alcove once held a spren in stasis -- nine are empty, their binding mechanisms shattered. The tenth still hums with Investiture, its occupant visible as a shimmer of light trapped in a lattice of ancient fabrial-work. The spren inside has been waiting a very long time.

npcs.name: Bound Spren (Honorspren, ancient)
npcs.dialogue: I was placed here by the last generation of Radiants -- not as punishment, but as preservation. They knew the Recreance was coming. They knew the bonds would break. I was to carry a message forward. It has been four thousand years. May I deliver it now?

event_triggers: On entry: The bound spren flares bright enough to cast shadows through solid stone. Nine empty alcoves. Nine broken bonds. One message preserved across four millennia.

content_hints.description: The Abandoned Forge still holds heat -- not from fire, but from Investiture channeled through fabrial conduits in the walls. The anvil is carved from a single gemstone, its surface scarred by thousands of hammer strikes. Molds for Shardplate components line the walls, most shattered, but a few intact. This is where the Radiants armed themselves.
event_triggers: On entry: The forge activates at your presence -- conduits in the walls pulse with Stormlight, and the temperature rises twenty degrees in seconds. The construct guardian stirs.

content_hints.description: The Honorblade Vault. A sealed chamber behind a door that responds only to Stormlight of precisely calibrated frequency. Inside, a single pedestal holds the imprint of a blade -- the Honorblade that was kept here has been removed, recently, the dust pattern still sharp. But the vault was not empty. A second pedestal, hidden behind the first, holds something the Stone Shamans never mentioned.
event_triggers: On entry: The vault's interior glows with residual Stormlight from the missing Honorblade. On the second pedestal, the unknown blade vibrates at a frequency that makes your Stormlight-infused spheres go dark, one by one.

content_hints.description: The Guardian Construct Chamber. The room was designed as a final defense -- a sphere of seamless stone with no corners and no exits save the one you entered. At its center, a construct of ancient design unfolds from a resting configuration: twelve feet of articulated stone limbs, gemstone eyes, and Surgebinding capabilities woven into its very structure. It was built to stop the Voidbringers. It does not distinguish between threats.
event_triggers: On entry: The construct's gemstone eyes cycle through ten colors -- one for each Radiant Order -- before settling on red. Red was not one of the original ten. Someone has modified this construct since it was built.
